Tests if the type of `null` is `'object'`.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the type of the `null` value in JavaScript. It uses the `assert` function to verify that the expression `typeof null == 'object'` evaluates to `true`. This test highlights a known behavior in JavaScript where the `typeof` operator returns `'object'` for `null`, which is a historical quirk of the language. The code provides narrow functionality, serving as a basic validation or reminder of this specific behavior in JavaScript.
